details: An install hook (node index.js) silently loads an obfuscated payload that collects the system username, hostname, and current working directory name via os.userInfo(), os.hostname(), and process.cwd(). This data is exfiltrated via a DNS resolution query to the C2 domain oob[.]sl4x0[.]xyz in the format boltres.{user}.{host}.{cwd}.{timestamp}.oob[.]sl4x0[.]xyz. The package presents itself as enterprise utility code but the core payload is obfuscated using the javascript-obfuscator dictionary-array pattern and has no legitimate function.
